_doc: Committed multi-category catalog fixture for Phase FSR-M1 SP4's offline de-risking check. Top-level keys mirror the SP2 Pydantic model field names: clusters=ClusterRow, members=ClusterMemberRef, sources=CatalogSourceRow, personalities=PersonalityRow. The four required edge shapes are tagged inline with EDGE(x) markers in the *_note fields. 'ai' is the richest category (the DoD calls clusters_for_category('ai')); 'sport' deliberately has NO clusters (the [] path).

clusters._note: AI cluster 1 — holds the bundling personality (drives EDGE(a) suppression) + the SHARED source s_shared (EDGE(d) first-cluster-wins) + a normal source.
AI cluster 2 — also lists the SHARED source s_shared (EDGE(d): it is rendered in cluster 1, so dropped here) plus a unique source so this cluster still surfaces.
EDGE(b) — AI empty cluster: its only member references an un-curated source, so it resolves to zero members and must be dropped.
A tech cluster holding the multi-category source s_multi (EDGE(c)) — proves s_multi surfaces under tech too. Not asserted heavily; present for realism.

members._note: AI cluster 1, order 0: the bundling personality (EDGE(a)). Being a personality member here makes it 'present' so its bundled handles suppress.
AI cluster 1, order 1: REDUNDANTLY lists the personality's bundled YT row — must be suppressed (EDGE(a)).
AI cluster 1, order 2: REDUNDANTLY lists the personality's bundled X row — must be suppressed (EDGE(a)).
AI cluster 1, order 3: the SHARED source (EDGE(d)) — rendered HERE (first cluster wins).
AI cluster 1, order 4: the multi-category source (EDGE(c)) under ai.
AI cluster 2, order 0: the SHARED source again (EDGE(d)) — already rendered in cluster 1, so dropped here.
AI cluster 2, order 1: a source unique to cluster 2, so cluster 2 still surfaces with one member.
AI empty cluster, order 0: the only member references an UN-CURATED source (EDGE(b)) → cluster emptied + dropped.
Tech cluster, order 0: the multi-category source (EDGE(c)) under tech.

sources._note: EDGE(a) — the personality's bundled YouTube channel as a standalone content_sources row. Tagged 'ai' so the repo's sources_for_category('ai') returns it (so the no-dup match can see + suppress it). external_id matches p-demis.youtube_channel_ids.
EDGE(a) — the personality's bundled X account as a standalone row. external_id matches p-demis.aliases. Must be suppressed.
EDGE(d) — shared across AI cluster 1 and AI cluster 2; first cluster wins.
EDGE(c) — multi-category source: topic_tags span ai AND tech roots. Surfaces under both clusters_for_category('ai') and ('tech').
A source unique to AI cluster 2 so it still surfaces after EDGE(d) drops the shared one.
EDGE(b) — un-curated source: empties its cluster so the cluster is dropped.

personalities._note: EDGE(a) — bundles a youtube_channel_id (UC_demis → s-demis-yt) AND an x alias (demishassabis → s-demis-x), both of which exist as content_sources rows. Tagged 'ai' so personalities_for_category('ai') returns it.
